LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, NOV-30-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT WHELAN SECURITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE FAIR CREDIT REPORTING ACT IS OVERRULED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KEVIN F. WOODALL, Esq. of Woodall Law Offices (Tel: 415-413-4629) appearing for plaintiff Michael Anthony Goto;
Atty. KEVIN SULLIVAN, Esq. of Epstein Becker & Green, P.C. (Tel: 310-557-9576) appearing for defendant Whelan Security of California, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WHELAN SECURITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to strike is denied. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:

APEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KEVIN F. WOODALL, Esq. of Woodall Law Offices (Tel: 415-413-4629) appearing for plaintiff Michael Anthony Goto;
Atty. KEVIN SULLIVAN, Esq. of Epstein Becker & Green, P.C. (Tel: 310-557-9576) appearing for defendant Whelan Security of California, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WHELAN SECURITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's demurrer to the ninth cause of action for violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act is overruled. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.
